title: Cross-Platform & IoT Frontend: Building Connected Experiences
summary: Harness your Flutter, Raspberry Pi, and IoT skills to build engaging connected frontend apps bridging physical and digital worlds for Truffaire.
personalizationNote: Builds on Sudarshan’s Flutter, Raspberry Pi, Android Automotive OS, and IoT project expertise, focusing on seamless frontend integration of physical sensors and services.
videoStyle: Mixed media with hardware photos and Flutter app demos
learningObjectives: Develop Flutter UI for IoT device control and monitoring
Integrate real-time sensor data into frontend components
Implement offline-first and graceful degradation strategies
Use Android Automotive OS frontend features in embedded systems
knowledgeChecks: How can Flutter Streams improve real-time sensor data display?
What techniques ensure IoT app resilience during network drops?
Name key frontend considerations for Android Automotive OS apps.
narrationScript:
Flutter UI for Real-Time IoT Data and Control
Real-time is everything in IoT. Flutter’s streams let you listen to sensor data updates from your Raspberry Pi and update the UI instantly. The Bloc pattern keeps state organized as you send control commands and receive status back. Designing clear feedback loops means users feel in command of smart devices—a core experience you champion at Truffaire.

Offline-First and Resilience Strategies for IoT Frontends
No network? No problem. By caching recent sensor readings and queued commands locally, your Flutter app stays interactive. Smart reconnect logic tries silently, syncing all changes seamlessly when connectivity returns. Users never notice glitches, just a reliable experience on Truffaire devices, whatever the network looks like.

Android Automotive OS Frontend Features and Integration
In cars, frontends must be responsive and lightweight. Implement layouts that adapt to vertical and wide screens. Media controls and voice commands keep users focused and safe. Efficient code ensures smooth operation within automotive hardware limits — skills you’re uniquely positioned to deliver from your IISc and PESU projects for Truffaire’s future vehicles.

Gracefully handle network failures and reconnect logic
Sync states when connection restores to keep UI consistent
slides.speakerNotes: IoT networks can be flaky. Your app must keep working smoothly at Truffaire, even offline, syncing data smartly when back online.

bullets: Implement responsive layouts for varied screen sizes
Integrate media controls and voice commands within frontends
Optimize resource usage for automotive hardware constraints
slides.speakerNotes: Your Raspberry Pi and Android Automotive OS work means you can raise Truffaire’s in-vehicle infotainment systems to the next level with responsive, resource-smart frontend features.
